name: Southwestern White Chili
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
2. Add the chicken and cook for 4 to 5 minutes, or until browned on all sides. Remove the chicken from the skillet and set aside, keeping it warm.
3. Add the chopped onion to the same skillet and cook for 2 minutes, or until softened.
4. Stir in the chopped green chiles, garlic powder, cumin, oregano, cilantro, and ground red pepper.
5. Pour in the chicken broth and add the white kidney beans (both undrain and Cannellini varieties).
6. Bring the mixture to a simmer, then reduce the heat to low and let it cook for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
7. Return the cooked chicken to the skillet and stir until heated through.
8. Add the additional kidney beans and continue to simmer for an additional 5-10 minutes.
9. Taste and adjust seasoning if needed.
10. Serve hot, garnished with shredded Monterey Jack cheese.
